solutions.solution: I does not follow: inquiries are not made for positive developments, arrests etc. Hence, certain action is needed; III does not follow: because of course of action. A bumper crop may lead to problem of storage and better damage in storage, hence III follows.

I and II are obvious. III is an extreme action; it puts a check on people's privacy.

I does not follow because it is the job of the weather experts to give forecasts. They have only carried out their normal job and for carrying out an assigned job, awards cannot be given. III is rubbish.

If legal provisions have proved inadequate, will it means either or both will follow; one, that the provisions are not adequate (hence I follow) and two, that they are not being sincerely executed (hence II follows) in past could have been accepted but it carries the objectionable advising rebelling against the parents.
I follows. I does not follow; there may be six good candidates. III also to accept a problem as inevitable. This is a negative approach.
It is an extreme action. Suspension should be carried out only if the alleged should proved guilty.
No interference and our III is interfering in our affairs. Hence, III does not follow.
The statement focuses on girl dropouts and not total dropouts. Hence, I does not follow. III is an extreme action.

None of the actions is rational enough. I advocates sacking the official on mere accusation and without enquiry. II suggests ignoring the accusation without enquiry. III has the same flow as I has. None of the three appears to be recommended without at least a prima facie evidence.

People should know the truth about any disease. Further, they boycott leprosy patients which is not justified, particularly if leprosy is not contagious. So, I and II are justified courses of action but III is not a seeks to declare leprosy as a risk-free disease. But only some, not all, forms of leprosy are non-contagious. Some of them are contagious and hence it can't be declared risk-free.

I and II are proper courses of action because both of these are time-tested 'treatments' for recession. III does not follow because it promotes escapism. Recession is an integral part of business and all businesses are cyclic in nature. It makes no sense to abandon a business simply because of demand has slumped.
